petitioner_background: The petitioner is self-represented and sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. The decision does not specify the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or any details about their career or qualifications. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 05 156 50723).
outcome_reasoning: The AAO summarily dismissed the appeal because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). The appellate submission contained only a general statement requesting reconsideration and was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3). The petitioner indicated he was not submitting a separate brief or evidence, did not specifically address the reasons stated for denial, and provided no additional evidence to overcome the director's determination that sustained national or international acclaim had not been established.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: The appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. The petitioner did not submit a brief or additional evidence on appeal.
